message:
beads: close mirror install, validate --fix, and asciicast stub issues

Close br-h8lsa.2 (mirror install), br-h8lsa.3 (analytics validate --fix),
and br-h8lsa.4 (asciicast stub) as completed. Update dependency chain
status for remaining open issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -861,9 +861,9 @@
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.1","title":"Record explicit user permission or fallback strategy for deleting legacy UI shell files","description":"## What

This is the explicit blocker for any task that would delete `src/ui/tui.rs`, `src/ui/components/help_strip.rs`, or `src/ui/components/widgets.rs`.

## Why It Exists

`AGENTS.md` forbids file deletion without explicit written user permission. The legacy-shell cleanup tasks are technically sound, but they cannot proceed until that permission is recorded.

## Completion Conditions

Close this bead only when one of the following is true:
1. The user gives explicit written permission to delete the affected files, and that permission is quoted in the close reason or notes.
2. The deletion tasks are re-scoped to a non-deletion fallback and no file removal will occur.

## Notes

This is intentionally a user/owner-gated task, not an engineering guess. It exists so the dependency graph tells the truth about what is and is not executable.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- The deletion boundary is explicitly represented in Beads.
- No file-deletion task becomes ready unless the permission or fallback condition has been satisfied.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:02.056472939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:31:19.060587359Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.10","title":"Add automated incomplete-code audit and CI guardrails for placeholder regressions","description":"## What

Add an automated audit that scans `src/` and other relevant production surfaces for incomplete-code markers and policy violations so the manual placeholder hunt does not need to be repeated from scratch.

## Scope

At minimum, the guard should detect or gate on:
- `not yet implemented`, `placeholder implementation`, `todo!`, `unimplemented!`, and similar high-signal markers in production code
- user-facing flags/help text that advertise features which are ignored or hard-error as unimplemented
- intentionally retained tiny files that are not classified as wrappers or legacy shells
- production model manifests with placeholder checksum sentinels

## Design Constraints

- Use a small allowlist for sanctioned shims/test scaffolding.
- Fail loudly on new ambiguous production placeholders.
- Keep the guard deterministic and cheap enough to run regularly.

## Testing

Add tests or CI-facing audit coverage proving that known sanctioned cases are allowlisted and new unsanctioned markers are caught.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- The repo contains an automated placeholder/incomplete-code audit with a clear allowlist.
- New unsanctioned production placeholders would fail the guard.
- The guard encodes the classification decisions made by the other beads in this epic.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:04.114328242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:31:26.070709675Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:31:25.257903942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:31:25.462671068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:31:25.665785639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:31:25.865278886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:31:26.069676820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.2","title":"Implement models install --mirror with verified mirror resolution and recovery semantics","description":"## What

Implement the currently unimplemented `cass models install --mirror ...` path in `src/lib.rs` and the supporting model-download stack.

## Scope

The mirror path must be a first-class install path, not a best-effort shortcut. It should preserve the same integrity and recovery guarantees as the normal installer:
- deterministic mirror URL resolution
- checksum verification
- revision or manifest identity preservation
- atomic staged install and verified-marker behavior
- actionable errors on missing/corrupt mirror artifacts
- truthful JSON/robot output

## Design Constraints

- Do not create a second-class code path that bypasses the existing installer invariants.
- Reuse the same manifest, checksum, resume, and staging logic wherever possible.
- Treat mirror configuration as transport/location selection, not as a waiver of integrity policy.
- Preserve lexical fail-open behavior when model install fails.

## Testing

Add comprehensive tests with detailed logs for:
- happy-path mirror install
- malformed mirror URL or path
- missing mirror artifact
- checksum mismatch or corrupted artifact
- interrupted install recovery / rerun behavior
- robot-mode output and error codes","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `cass models install --mirror ...` no longer hard-errors as unimplemented.
- Mirror installs honor the same integrity checks and atomicity guarantees as the default install path.
- Targeted unit/integration/CLI tests cover success, corruption, missing artifact, and recovery cases with useful logging.
- Cargo verification for the touched slice is run through `rch`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:02.278748075Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:47:17.045603104Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T23:47:17.045321307Z","close_reason":"Implemented --mirror as a first-class install path with validated HTTP(S) base URLs, shared checksum/atomic install invariants, mirror-aware CLI handling, and rch-verified unit+CLI coverage for success, invalid URL, 404, corruption, and cancel/resume recovery.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.3","title":"Implement analytics validate --fix with safe repair classes and truthful reporting","description":"## What

Implement the advertised-but-ignored `cass analytics validate --fix` behavior so the flag performs real, bounded repair work instead of being silently ignored.

## Scope

The implementation must classify validation findings into at least these buckets:
- safe auto-fixable derived state
- rebuildable but potentially expensive state
- not safely fixable / operator-action-required corruption

The command output must be truthful about which findings were detected, which were fixed, which were skipped, and why.

## Design Constraints

- No destructive repair-by-default that could compromise canonical data.
- Prefer rebuilding derived analytics state from canonical sources where possible.
- If some findings cannot be fixed safely, the CLI must say so explicitly rather than implying full repair capability.
- Preserve existing non-fix validation behavior for users who do not pass `--fix`.

## Testing

Add comprehensive unit/integration/CLI coverage with detailed logs for:
- healthy dataset + `--fix` is a no-op
- fixable derived-state repair
- partially fixable dataset with explicit skipped reasons
- non-fixable corruption refusal
- idempotent repeated runs
- truthful robot/JSON reporting of what changed","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `analytics validate --fix` performs real repairs where safe and reports limits truthfully.
- No silently ignored `--fix` flag remains.
- Tests cover healthy, fixable, partially fixable, and non-fixable cases with preserved logs/artifacts.
- Cargo verification for the touched slice is run through `rch`.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:02.501079195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:47:17.233902317Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.4","title":"Replace headless asciicast stub output with a truthful recording or truthful sentinel artifact","description":"## What

Resolve the headless asciicast path that currently writes a vacuous stub payload instead of a truthful recording of what happened.

## Scope

The final behavior must do one of two things, explicitly and honestly:
1. Produce a real asciicast using the genuine recording machinery, with valid timing and non-vacuous frames when terminal activity occurs.
2. If real recording is not possible in that mode, stop pretending it is a recording and emit a clearly labeled sentinel/metadata artifact instead.

## Design Constraints

- Do not keep shipping a file that looks like a real recording but contains only stub content.
- Reuse the canonical asciicast schema/writer path if real recording is implemented.
- Robot/JSON output must accurately describe what artifact was produced.

## Testing

Add comprehensive tests with detailed logging for:
- real headless run produces valid recording structure
- activity yields non-empty frames when recording is enabled
- unavailable-recording mode yields a truthful sentinel artifact, not a fake recording
- overwrite/retry/error behavior is explicit and deterministic","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- No fake or vacuous asciicast file is emitted while being described as a recording.
- The chosen behavior is truthful in both data and CLI/robot metadata.
- Targeted tests validate structure, non-vacuity, and failure-path reporting.
- Cargo verification for the touched slice is run through `rch`.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:02.709943237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:31:20.691799177Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.5","title":"Clean stale implemented-feature comments, help text, and tests that still claim placeholders","description":"## What

Remove stale wording and stale tests that still describe implemented behavior as unimplemented or placeholder behavior.

## Initial Known Targets

- stale analytics "not yet implemented" commentary in `src/lib.rs`
- stale `encrypt_content()` placeholder wording beyond the dedicated crypto doc-comment fix if any remain
- stale tests that still assume `models install --from-file` is unimplemented
- any CLI/help or robot docs that still claim an implemented feature is unavailable

## Design Constraints

- Do not paper over real gaps. If a feature is genuinely incomplete, keep it explicit.
- This task is about restoring truthfulness where implementation has already landed.
- Prefer updating or replacing stale tests with real behavioral coverage instead of merely deleting expectations.

## Testing

Add or update targeted tests so the docs/help/test expectations match actual behavior. Preserve concrete evidence for every corrected stale claim.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- No stale implemented-feature claim remains in code comments, help text, or focused tests within the audited scope.
- Existing features are verified by real tests rather than placeholder expectations.
- Corrections are narrow and truthful, not sweeping or speculative.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:02.929567356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:31:21.249099562Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

- Tests cover healthy, fixable, partially fixable, and non-fixable cases with preserved logs/artifacts.
- Cargo verification for the touched slice is run through `rch`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:02.501079195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:40.404492751Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:40.404149788Z","close_reason":"Implemented analytics validate --fix with deep validation, safe Track A rebuilds, explicit skipped non-fixable repairs, robust post-fix reporting, frankensqlite-safe cross-track validation, corrected API-only rollup accounting, and comprehensive regression coverage; verified via rch with api_rollups_require_api_data_source, repair_plan_, analytics_validate_ slices, and cargo check --all-targets.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.4","title":"Replace headless asciicast stub output with a truthful recording or truthful sentinel artifact","description":"## What

Add or update targeted tests so the docs/help/test expectations match actual behavior. Preserve concrete evidence for every corrected stale claim.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- No stale implemented-feature claim remains in code comments, help text, or focused tests within the audited scope.
- Existing features are verified by real tests rather than placeholder expectations.
- Corrections are narrow and truthful, not sweeping or speculative.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:02.929567356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:03:21.303807614Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.6","title":"Classify intentional compatibility shims, disabled paths, and legacy shells so they stop looking accidental","description":"## What

Audit and reclassify the intentionally retained tiny modules, compatibility re-exports, disabled fallbacks, and no-op daemon paths so they are clearly deliberate and test-backed.

## Scope

This includes, at minimum:
- connector/search compatibility re-export wrappers
- the legacy shell files under `src/ui/` if they are retained instead of deleted
- `search_sqlite_fts5()` disabled fallback truthfulness
- `include_attachments` unimplemented-but-exposed behavior
- OPFS migration early-return semantics
- `NoopDaemonClient` or equivalent degraded-mode surfaces

## Design Constraints

- The goal is not to delete everything small; the goal is to distinguish intentional shells from accidental placeholders.
- User-visible disabled paths must expose truthful status and next-step guidance.
- If a legacy shell is retained because deletion permission is unavailable, it must be explicitly documented as retained by policy rather than by accident.

## Testing

Add focused unit/CLI/help tests where applicable so future audits can distinguish sanctioned shims from regressions.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Intentional shims and disabled paths are unmistakably documented and, where relevant, covered by targeted tests.
- Retained legacy shells no longer look like forgotten implementations.
- User-facing degraded-mode behavior is truthful and reproducible.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:03.144615994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:31:21.795949188Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.7","title":"Replace silent platform durability no-ops with explicit capability-aware behavior","description":"## What

Unify the scattered platform-specific durability helpers that silently return success on unsupported platforms and replace them with an explicit capability model.

## Scope

The audit found multiple helpers across storage, indexer, model download, UI, pages, and sources layers that effectively do `Ok(())` on some platforms. Those may be intentional, but they should not masquerade as full durability.

## Design Constraints

- Introduce a shared capability model such as `fully_durable`, `best_effort`, or `unsupported`.
- Callers must not silently assume they got stronger persistence guarantees than the platform can provide.
- Preserve current successful behavior on supported platforms while making downgraded guarantees explicit.

## Testing

Add targeted tests for supported and downgraded capability paths plus clear logging/status surfaces where capability downgrades matter.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Silent success on unsupported durability paths is eliminated or made explicitly capability-scoped.
- Callers can distinguish full durability from best-effort behavior.
- Tests cover at least one strong path and one downgraded path.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:03.368465058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:31:22.347993467Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-h8lsa.8","title":"Isolate and rename test-only doubles and placeholder fixtures so audits can exclude them safely","description":"## What

Make test-only mocks, fakes, fault injectors, and placeholder fixture artifacts unmistakably non-production so future repo-wide placeholder audits have a clean allowlist surface.

## Scope

This includes fixture daemons, fault-injection connectors, fake installer artifacts, and any production-adjacent test helpers whose names currently blur the line between real and fake behavior.

## Design Constraints

- Prefer `#[cfg(test)]`, `tests/`, and explicit names like `Fixture*`, `FaultInjection*`, or `Test*`.
- Do not break legitimate test coverage.
- Leave production behavior untouched except where better isolation is needed.

## Testing

Add compile/test coverage that proves the renamed or relocated doubles still serve their tests and do not leak into normal builds.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Test-only doubles are clearly named and isolated.
- Placeholder audits can distinguish sanctioned test scaffolding from accidental production stubs.
- Existing tests that depend on the doubles continue to pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:03.591169518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:31:22.906136089Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
